name: TWIN STAR INN
detail: It’s hard to beat this level of accessibility in Taipei.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Poshpacker Hotel
detail: I stayed at women's floor. Among lots of hotel in Taipei, the most important thing to me is the cleaness and easy access to station. This hotel is not only good location but also it was very clean and tidy. The room & bed was compact, but to me, there was no problem. I could stay so pleasantly. The first floor is coffee shop; wake up, go down stair, get a cup of offee easily, it is very nice. Around the hotel, there are food shops, book store and department store. You have got lots options to go&eat.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Grand Hotel
detail: Extraordinary architecture of the Hotel is worth capturing in your camera. Bathrooms are thoroughly clean with everything at right place with right condition. Also the room comes with Huge balcony which was gift to me. As I am smoker so was not able to get any Hotel in Taipei where smoking is allowed. Then found out that smoking is prohibited by law in Hotel rooms. So this balcony came as blessing in disguise.

name: Roaders Plus Hotel Taipei Station
detail: The laundry facility was great, clean and well-managed.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Taipei Fullerton Hotel - South
detail: Taipei hotels are expensive to begin with, so this is not an exception. Location is great, less than 100 metres away from Da-an MRT. Surrounded by eating places. Rooms are modern and very clean. Small but for a solo traveller or couple, can seem cozy. This hotel is geared primarily to the business traveller, but thankfully, not as formal and intimidating. The best thing is that you will not find bus loads of tourists here.

name: Finders Hotel Fu Qian
detail: I have to say that, this, by far one of the most affordable and most convenient hotel in Taipei. It is walking distance from train stations, hawkers area are all nearby and their lobby is very homey. All their staffs are very accommodating. Even though we don’t know how to speak in Chinese, they try their best to explain or answer our questions. KUDOS to this hotel and their employees.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Miramar Garden Hotel
detail: Though 5 star hotel, it is reasonable price, but staff are worthy of 5 stars, very courteous and kind. Rooms are clean, relaxing with high ceiling, dignified corridor. Breakfast was delicious while we could enjoy beautiful Asian garden. Especially I like breads and pan cakes in this hotel. This time, we experienced flight cancel due to typhoon and had been in troubled for contacting with airline company, and the staff was willingly trying to understand what I wanted to say from my poor English , though the reception counter was busy, she made every effort to support me, which was very impressive and I really appreciate her. It is a little from from Taipei station, but it is no inconvenient if taking a taxi ( taxi fare is low in Taiwan). Rather, a little far from busy street, we were able to stay comfortably, calmly. I think this hotel is reliable and good enough to stay repeatedly.

name: MGH Mitsui Garden Hotel Taipei Zhongxiao
detail: I can't believe there's less than 5MB internet in the room. Nowadays internet is a must for business travelers, yet this is the worst WIFI I had around new hotels Taipei city...

name: We Come Hostel
detail: Stayed here for two nights, supposedly just for a night. The staff was really helpful to arrange me one bed for two night since my booking for two nights is two different separate booking. The hostel is just a few minutes walk from the Airport MRT. So if you are coming to other parts of Taiwan via HSR or TRA, just look for the Airport MRT and exit the Y18. From there, just go straight and turn left after the police station. To be honest, I thought that the hostel is nearer to Taipei Main Station, but it's actually nearer Beimen Station. From the hostel also, you can walk yourself going to Ximending. Their door closes at 11PM, so be sure to arrive hostel before that. Also, the place is a refurbished office space, I think. Their lobby is at 2nd floor. Breakfast also was great with coffee, bread and jams, and sometimes nuggets and fries. I must say their bed is really comfy as compared to other dorm beds I have slept in, so five star for that.

name: NYS LOFT HOTEL
detail: We went in group of 4 (3 friends and an elder mother) in a 7-day trip to Taiwan, with first three days staying at NYS LOFT HOTEL in Taipei. I would say that this staying goes beyonds our expectation for a three-star hotel. (1) Nice and helpful staff with fluent English. They even helped us exchange money at late night when we run out of cash and needed to go early on the following morning to Chiayi. They also allowed us to leave our big luggage at the checkin counter during 2 days we were on our trip to Chiayi. (2) Utilities: Free umbrellas to use whenever you need. Free coffee, tea (on self-serving). Spacious common room for kitchen and living room, with oven, glasses, dishes, and bows (wash yourselves after use). (3) Room services: quite spacious (in the description it writes 9 square meters for double room, but I think it is bigger. All facilities are modern and work well (hot water, air conditioner, mini fridge, mini bar, hair-dresser, etc.) and convenient to use; quiet and clean. We booked 2 rooms for 4 people, and both the rooms are excellent. (4) Location: excellent since it takes only 3 minutes walking to the City Main station. The neighbourhood is packed with lots of local shops, foodstores, convenient stores (7-11 stores, family marts, Life OK,etc. which open 24/7). I definitely book this hotel again for my next trips to Taipei as well as recommend it to my friends :)
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map
